{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01UMbVr36QdbspzJ5XR6dkPh","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_018v6485E6dJsfwfXq3Q3J23","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/notifications/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3169,"cache_read_input_tokens":16911,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":3169,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":25,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01C53y9Uu5Cyt8xgnYwq99Ev","session_id":"282376ca-7a0b-4c20-878b-0086a85b681b","uuid":"9fca9ac2-dbb7-4cf5-b794-f77239f33338"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_018v6485E6dJsfwfXq3Q3J23","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→export async function GET() {\n     6→  const session = await requireAuth();\n     7→  if (!session) {\n     8→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n     9→  }\n    10→\n    11→  const notifications = db\n    12→    .prepare(\n    13→      `SELECT n.*, l.book_title\n    14→       FROM notifications n\n    15→       LEFT JOIN listings l ON n.listing_id = l.id\n    16→       WHERE n.user_id = ?\n    17→       ORDER BY n.created_at DESC\n    18→       LIMIT 50`\n    19→    )\n    20→    .all(session.userId);\n    21→\n    22→  const unreadCount = db\n    23→    .prepare(\n    24→      \"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM notifications WHERE user_id = ? AND is_read = 0\"\n    25→    )\n    26→    .get(session.userId) as { count: number };\n    27→\n    28→  return NextResponse.json({\n    29→    notifications,\n    30→    unreadCount: unreadCount.count,\n    31→  });\n    32→}\n    33→\n    34→export async function PATCH(req: NextRequest) {\n    35→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    36→  if (!session) {\n    37→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    38→  }\n    39→\n    40→  const { notificationId } = await req.json();\n    41→\n    42→  if (notificationId) {\n    43→    db.prepare(\n    44→      \"UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1 WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    45→    ).run(notificationId, session.userId);\n    46→  } else {\n    47→    // Mark all as read\n    48→    db.prepare(\n    49→      \"UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1 WHERE user_id = ?\"\n    50→    ).run(session.userId);\n    51→  }\n    52→\n    53→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n    54→}\n    55→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01C53y9Uu5Cyt8xgnYwq99Ev","session_id":"282376ca-7a0b-4c20-878b-0086a85b681b","uuid":"8765ddc7-85f2-442a-88c1-847b63e1fbda","timestamp":"2026-03-20T11:02:11.048Z"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"task_progress","task_id":"a6c6355156d4cb6f6","tool_use_id":"toolu_01C53y9Uu5Cyt8xgnYwq99Ev","description":"Reading app/app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts","usage":{"total_tokens":20420,"tool_uses":16,"duration_ms":25835},"last_tool_name":"Read","uuid":"ec9a3afc-137b-460c-b651-a5df353474c3","session_id":"282376ca-7a0b-4c20-878b-0086a85b681b"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01UMbVr36QdbspzJ5XR6dkPh","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_0178q8qnp7PaPqK5PPnJjQuz","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3169,"cache_read_input_tokens":16911,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":3169,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":25,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01C53y9Uu5Cyt8xgnYwq99Ev","session_id":"282376ca-7a0b-4c20-878b-0086a85b681b","uuid":"83ed5f23-a836-468a-9572-af8ccadd1a50"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_0178q8qnp7PaPqK5PPnJjQuz","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import {\n     4→  createChatInviteLink,\n     5→  exportChatInviteLink,\n     6→  sendMessage,\n     7→  parseListingIdFromPayload,\n     8→} from \"@/lib/telegram\";\n     9→import { createNotification } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n    10→\n    11→/**\n    12→ * Telegram webhook endpoint.\n    13→ *\n    14→ * Handles two types of updates:\n    15→ *\n    16→ * 1. `my_chat_member` — bot was added to a group\n    17→ *    When the author creates a group via the deep link, the bot is\n    18→ *    automatically added. We detect this, generate an invite link,\n    19→ *    and save it to the listing.\n    20→ *\n    21→ * 2. `message` with `/start listing_ID` — the bot receives a start\n    22→ *    command in a group with a payload referencing a listing.\n    23→ */\n    24→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n    25→  // Verify webhook secret if configured\n    26→  const webhookSecret = process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET;\n    27→  if (webhookSecret) {\n    28→    const headerSecret = req.headers.get(\"X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token\");\n    29→    if (headerSecret !== webhookSecret) {\n    30→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unauthorized\" }, { status: 403 });\n    31→    }\n    32→  }\n    33→\n    34→  let update: Record<string, unknown>;\n    35→  try {\n    36→    update = await req.json();\n    37→  } catch {\n    38→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid JSON\" }, { status: 400 });\n    39→  }\n    40→\n    41→  // Handle bot being added to a group chat\n    42→  if (update.my_chat_member) {\n    43→    await handleChatMemberUpdate(\n    44→      update.my_chat_member as ChatMemberUpdate\n    45→    );\n    46→  }\n    47→\n    48→  // Handle /start command in groups (fallback mechanism)\n    49→  if (update.message) {\n    50→    await handleMessage(update.message as TelegramMessage);\n    51→  }\n    52→\n    53→  // Always return 200 to Telegram to avoid retries\n    54→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n    55→}\n    56→\n    57→interface ChatMemberUpdate {\n    58→  chat: {\n    59→    id: number;\n    60→    title?: string;\n    61→    type: string;\n    62→  };\n    63→  from: {\n    64→    id: number;\n    65→    first_name: string;\n    66→  };\n    67→  new_chat_member: {\n    68→    user: {\n    69→      id: number;\n    70→      is_bot: boolean;\n    71→    };\n    72→    status: string;\n    73→  };\n    74→}\n    75→\n    76→interface TelegramMessage {\n    77→  chat: {\n    78→    id: number;\n    79→    type: string;\n    80→    title?: string;\n    81→  };\n    82→  text?: string;\n    83→  from?: {\n    84→    id: number;\n    85→  };\n    86→}\n    87→\n    88→/**\n    89→ * When the bot is added to a group, check if it's related to a listing.\n    90→ *\n    91→ * The deep link format is: t.me/BOT?startgroup=listing_ID\n    92→ * When a user creates a group through this link, Telegram:\n    93→ * 1. Creates the group\n    94→ * 2. Adds the bot as a member\n    95→ * 3. Sends a `my_chat_member` update\n    96→ *\n    97→ * We use the group title or a pending mapping to associate the chat with a listing.\n    98→ */\n    99→async function handleChatMemberUpdate(update: ChatMemberUpdate) {\n   100→  const { chat, new_chat_member } = update;\n   101→\n   102→  // Only process when bot is added (member or administrator)\n   103→  if (\n   104→    !new_chat_member.user.is_bot ||\n   105→    ![\"member\", \"administrator\"].includes(new_chat_member.status)\n   106→  ) {\n   107→    return;\n   108→  }\n   109→\n   110→  // Only process group chats\n   111→  if (![\"group\", \"supergroup\"].includes(chat.type)) {\n   112→    return;\n   113→  }\n   114→\n   115→  // Try to find the listing this group belongs to.\n   116→  // Strategy 1: Check pending_telegram_groups mapping table\n   117→  const pending = db\n   118→    .prepare(\n   119→      \"SELECT listing_id FROM pending_telegram_groups WHERE telegram_chat_id = ?\"\n   120→    )\n   121→    .get(chat.id) as { listing_id: number } | undefined;\n   122→\n   123→  if (pending) {\n   124→    await linkTelegramToListing(pending.listing_id, chat.id);\n   125→    db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM pending_telegram_groups WHERE telegram_chat_id = ?\").run(\n   126→      chat.id\n   127→    );\n   128→    return;\n   129→  }\n   130→\n   131→  // Strategy 2: Check for listings awaiting auto-telegram (most recent unlinked full listing by this author)\n   132→  // We store the chat ID and wait for the /start command or title match\n   133→  // For now, store the mapping and send a welcome message\n   134→  await sendMessage(\n   135→    chat.id,\n   136→    \"Hello! I'm the Bookmate bot. I'll help set up this reading group.\\n\\n\" +\n   137→      'If this group was created for a Bookmate listing, I\\'ll automatically generate an invite link. ' +\n   138→      \"The organizer can also type <code>/link</code> to manually trigger the setup.\"\n   139→  );\n   140→\n   141→  // Store the chat for potential later linking\n   142→  try {\n   143→    db.prepare(\n   144→      \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO telegram_chats (chat_id, chat_title, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, datetime('now'))\"\n   145→    ).run(chat.id, chat.title || \"\");\n   146→  } catch {\n   147→    // Table might not exist yet on first run — handled gracefully\n   148→  }\n   149→}\n   150→\n   151→/**\n   152→ * Handle messages in group chats.\n   153→ * Supports /start with payload and /link command.\n   154→ */\n   155→async function handleMessage(message: TelegramMessage) {\n   156→  if (!message.text || ![\"group\", \"supergroup\"].includes(message.chat.type)) {\n   157→    return;\n   158→  }\n   159→\n   160→  const text = message.text.trim();\n   161→\n   162→  // Handle /start with listing payload (sent when group is created via deep link)\n   163→  if (text.startsWith(\"/start \")) {\n   164→    const payload = text.replace(\"/start \", \"\").trim();\n   165→    const listingId = parseListingIdFromPayload(payload);\n   166→    if (listingId) {\n   167→      await linkTelegramToListing(listingId, message.chat.id);\n   168→      return;\n   169→    }\n   170→  }\n   171→\n   172→  // Handle /link command — allow manual linking\n   173→  if (text === \"/link\") {\n   174→    // Find the most recent full listing without a telegram link\n   175→    // that was authored by someone (we can't verify Telegram user = Bookmate user\n   176→    // without account linking, so we just report available listings)\n   177→    const unlinkedListings = db\n   178→      .prepare(\n   179→        `SELECT id, book_title FROM listings\n   180→         WHERE is_full = 1 AND (telegram_link = '' OR telegram_link IS NULL)\n   181→         AND telegram_chat_id IS NULL\n   182→         ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 5`\n   183→      )\n   184→      .all() as { id: number; book_title: string }[];\n   185→\n   186→    if (unlinkedListings.length === 0) {\n   187→      await sendMessage(\n   188→        message.chat.id,\n   189→        \"No unlinked reading groups found. The group may already be set up!\"\n   190→      );\n   191→      return;\n   192→    }\n   193→\n   194→    if (unlinkedListings.length === 1) {\n   195→      // Auto-link the only available listing\n   196→      await linkTelegramToListing(unlinkedListings[0].id, message.chat.id);\n   197→      return;\n   198→    }\n   199→\n   200→    // Show options\n   201→    const listText = unlinkedListings\n   202→      .map((l) => `  /link_${l.id} — \"${l.book_title}\"`)\n   203→      .join(\"\\n\");\n   204→    await sendMessage(\n   205→      message.chat.id,\n   206→      `Multiple reading groups need a Telegram link. Pick one:\\n\\n${listText}`\n   207→    );\n   208→    return;\n   209→  }\n   210→\n   211→  // Handle /link_ID command\n   212→  const linkMatch = text.match(/^\\/link_(\\d+)$/);\n   213→  if (linkMatch) {\n   214→    const listingId = parseInt(linkMatch[1], 10);\n   215→    await linkTelegramToListing(listingId, message.chat.id);\n   216→  }\n   217→}\n   218→\n   219→/**\n   220→ * Link a Telegram chat to a listing:\n   221→ * 1. Generate an invite link\n   222→ * 2. Save it to the listing\n   223→ * 3. Notify all members\n   224→ * 4. Send a confirmation message to the Telegram group\n   225→ */\n   226→async function linkTelegramToListing(listingId: number, chatId: number) {\n   227→  const listing = db\n   228→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   229→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n   230→\n   231→  if (!listing) {\n   232→    await sendMessage(chatId, \"Could not find that reading group on Bookmate.\");\n   233→    return;\n   234→  }\n   235→\n   236→  if (listing.telegram_link) {\n   237→    await sendMessage(chatId, \"This reading group already has a Telegram link set up!\");\n   238→    return;\n   239→  }\n   240→\n   241→  // Try to create an invite link\n   242→  let inviteLink = await createChatInviteLink(chatId);\n   243→  if (!inviteLink) {\n   244→    // Fallback: export the default invite link\n   245→    inviteLink = await exportChatInviteLink(chatId);\n   246→  }\n   247→\n   248→  if (!inviteLink) {\n   249→    await sendMessage(\n   250→      chatId,\n   251→      \"I couldn't generate an invite link. Please make sure I have admin permissions in this group, \" +\n   252→        \"then try again with /link\"\n   253→    );\n   254→    return;\n   255→  }\n   256→\n   257→  // Save to database\n   258→  db.prepare(\n   259→    \"UPDATE listings SET telegram_link = ?, telegram_chat_id = ? WHERE id = ?\"\n   260→  ).run(inviteLink, chatId, listingId);\n   261→\n   262→  // Notify all members on Bookmate\n   263→  const members = db\n   264→    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   265→    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n   266→\n   267→  const bookTitle = listing.book_title as string;\n   268→  for (const member of members) {\n   269→    createNotification(\n   270→      member.user_id,\n   271→      listingId,\n   272→      \"telegram_ready\",\n   273→      `The Telegram group for \"${bookTitle}\" is ready! Click to view the invite link.`\n   274→    );\n   275→  }\n   276→\n   277→  // Send confirmation to the Telegram group\n   278→  await sendMessage(\n   279→    chatId,\n   280→    `This group is now linked to the Bookmate reading group for \"<b>${bookTitle}</b>\".\\n\\n` +\n   281→      `Invite link: ${inviteLink}\\n\\n` +\n   282→      \"All group members have been notified on Bookmate. Happy reading!\"\n   283→  );\n   284→}\n   285→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01C53y9Uu5Cyt8xgnYwq99Ev","session_id":"282376ca-7a0b-4c20-878b-0086a85b681b","uuid":"34ff1894-abdf-4501-bff1-dcc539f1f496","timestamp":"2026-03-20T11:02:11.485Z"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"task_progress","task_id":"a6c6355156d4cb6f6","tool_use_id":"toolu_01C53y9Uu5Cyt8xgnYwq99Ev","description":"Reading app/app/api/discord/webhook/route.ts","usage":{"total_tokens":26947,"tool_uses":17,"duration_ms":28822},"last_tool_name":"Read","uuid":"6906463e-de6e-46d5-a2e5-247081d1bb16","session_id":"282376ca-7a0b-4c20-878b-0086a85b681b"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_013GtBEwdFapcksRgmQ5dSiD","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01DBmn7MRzF2FTCG6thzaYqd","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/discord/webhook/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":6502,"cache_read_input_tokens":20080,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":6502,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":25,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01C53y9Uu5Cyt8xgnYwq99Ev","session_id":"282376ca-7a0b-4c20-878b-0086a85b681b","uuid":"0886a8b9-2b2c-41fb-a2d5-89f8bbc59d21"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01DBmn7MRzF2FTCG6thzaYqd","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import {\n     4→  createTextChannel,\n     5→  createChannelInvite,\n     6→  sendChannelMessage,\n     7→} from \"@/lib/discord\";\n     8→import { createNotification } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     9→\n    10→/**\n    11→ * Discord webhook endpoint for bot events.\n    12→ *\n    13→ * This endpoint handles:\n    14→ * 1. POST with { type: \"link\", guildId, listingId } - Link a Discord server to a listing\n    15→ *    (called by the bot or by an admin setup flow)\n    16→ *\n    17→ * Note: Unlike Telegram's push-based webhook, Discord uses Gateway (WebSocket)\n    18→ * for real-time events. This endpoint is used as an HTTP callback for\n    19→ * simpler integration scenarios or manual triggering.\n    20→ */\n    21→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n    22→  try {\n    23→    const body = await req.json();\n    24→    const { type } = body;\n    25→\n    26→    if (type === \"link\") {\n    27→      return handleLink(body);\n    28→    }\n    29→\n    30→    // Discord Interaction verification (for future slash command support)\n    31→    if (type === 1) {\n    32→      // PING - respond with PONG\n    33→      return NextResponse.json({ type: 1 });\n    34→    }\n    35→\n    36→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unknown event type\" }, { status: 400 });\n    37→  } catch (err) {\n    38→    console.error(\"Discord webhook error:\", err);\n    39→    return NextResponse.json(\n    40→      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n    41→      { status: 500 }\n    42→    );\n    43→  }\n    44→}\n    45→\n    46→async function handleLink(body: {\n    47→  guildId: string;\n    48→  listingId: number;\n    49→  channelId?: string;\n    50→}) {\n    51→  const { guildId, listingId, channelId } = body;\n    52→\n    53→  if (!guildId || !listingId) {\n    54→    return NextResponse.json(\n    55→      { error: \"Missing guildId or listingId\" },\n    56→      { status: 400 }\n    57→    );\n    58→  }\n    59→\n    60→  const listing = db\n    61→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    62→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    63→\n    64→  if (!listing) {\n    65→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    66→  }\n    67→\n    68→  if (listing.discord_link) {\n    69→    return NextResponse.json(\n    70→      { error: \"Discord link already set for this listing\" },\n    71→      { status: 400 }\n    72→    );\n    73→  }\n    74→\n    75→  let targetChannelId = channelId;\n    76→\n    77→  // If no channel specified, create one\n    78→  if (!targetChannelId) {\n    79→    const bookTitle = String(listing.book_title || \"reading-group\");\n    80→    const channelName = `bookmate-${bookTitle\n    81→      .toLowerCase()\n    82→      .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, \"-\")\n    83→      .slice(0, 80)}`;\n    84→\n    85→    const channel = await createTextChannel(\n    86→      guildId,\n    87→      channelName,\n    88→      `Bookmate reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" by ${listing.book_author}`\n    89→    );\n    90→\n    91→    if (!channel) {\n    92→      return NextResponse.json(\n    93→        { error: \"Failed to create Discord channel. Make sure the bot has Manage Channels permission.\" },\n    94→        { status: 500 }\n    95→      );\n    96→    }\n    97→\n    98→    targetChannelId = channel.id;\n    99→  }\n   100→\n   101→  // Create invite link\n   102→  const inviteLink = await createChannelInvite(targetChannelId);\n   103→  if (!inviteLink) {\n   104→    return NextResponse.json(\n   105→      { error: \"Failed to create invite link. Make sure the bot has Create Instant Invite permission.\" },\n   106→      { status: 500 }\n   107→    );\n   108→  }\n   109→\n   110→  // Save to DB\n   111→  db.prepare(\n   112→    \"UPDATE listings SET discord_link = ?, discord_channel_id = ? WHERE id = ?\"\n   113→  ).run(inviteLink, targetChannelId, listingId);\n   114→\n   115→  // Notify all members\n   116→  const members = db\n   117→    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   118→    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n   119→\n   120→  for (const member of members) {\n   121→    createNotification(\n   122→      member.user_id,\n   123→      listingId,\n   124→      \"discord_ready\",\n   125→      `The Discord channel for \"${listing.book_title}\" is ready! Join to start discussing.`\n   126→    );\n   127→  }\n   128→\n   129→  // Send welcome message to the channel\n   130→  await sendChannelMessage(\n   131→    targetChannelId,\n   132→    `Welcome to the **${listing.book_title}** reading group! 📚\\n\\n` +\n   133→      `This channel was created by Bookmate for your reading group.\\n` +\n   134→      `**Reading pace:** ${listing.reading_pace}\\n` +\n   135→      `**Start date:** ${listing.start_date}\\n\\n` +\n   136→      `Happy reading! 🎉`\n   137→  );\n   138→\n   139→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, inviteLink });\n   140→}\n   141→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01C53y9Uu5Cyt8xgnYwq99Ev","session_id":"282376ca-7a0b-4c20-878b-0086a85b681b","uuid":"cf9f3130-5828-407f-b92b-2d4774a9b617","timestamp":"2026-03-20T11:02:14.471Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01WChxbYQExCFwR4WTSaWq3M","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import db from \"./db\";\n     2→import { sendEmail } from \"./email\";\n     3→\n     4→function getUserEmail(userId: number): string | null {\n     5→  const user = db\n     6→    .prepare(\"SELECT email FROM users WHERE id = ?\")\n     7→    .get(userId) as { email: string } | undefined;\n     8→  return user?.email || null;\n     9→}\n    10→\n    11→export function createNotification(\n    12→  userId: number,\n    13→  listingId: number,\n    14→  type: string,\n    15→  message: string\n    16→) {\n    17→  db.prepare(\n    18→    \"INSERT INTO notifications (user_id, listing_id, type, message) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    19→  ).run(userId, listingId, type, message);\n    20→\n    21→  // Also send email notification (fire-and-forget)\n    22→  const email = getUserEmail(userId);\n    23→  if (email) {\n    24→    const subject = getEmailSubject(type);\n    25→    sendEmail(email, subject, message).catch(() => {\n    26→      // Email sending is best-effort — don't block on failure\n    27→    });\n    28→  }\n    29→}\n    30→\n    31→function getEmailSubject(type: string): string {\n    32→  switch (type) {\n    33→    case \"new_member\":\n    34→      return \"Bookmate: Someone joined your reading group!\";\n    35→    case \"group_full\":\n    36→      return \"Bookmate: Your reading group is full!\";\n    37→    case \"application_received\":\n    38→      return \"Bookmate: New application for your reading group\";\n    39→    case \"application_approved\":\n    40→      return \"Bookmate: Your application was approved!\";\n    41→    case \"application_rejected\":\n    42→      return \"Bookmate: Application update\";\n    43→    case \"rating_received\":\n    44→      return \"Bookmate: You received a new rating\";\n    45→    default:\n    46→      return \"Bookmate: Notification\";\n    47→  }\n    48→}\n    49→\n    50→export function notifyListingAuthor(\n    51→  listingId: number,\n    52→  joinerName: string\n    53→) {\n    54→  const listing = db\n    55→    .prepare(\n    56→      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    57→    )\n    58→    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n    59→\n    60→  if (listing) {\n    61→    createNotification(\n    62→      listing.author_id,\n    63→      listingId,\n    64→      \"new_member\",\n    65→      `${joinerName} joined your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    66→    );\n    67→  }\n    68→}\n    69→\n    70→export function notifyApplicationReceived(\n    71→  listingId: number,\n    72→  applicantName: string\n    73→) {\n    74→  const listing = db\n    75→    .prepare(\n    76→      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    77→    )\n    78→    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n    79→\n    80→  if (listing) {\n    81→    createNotification(\n    82→      listing.author_id,\n    83→      listingId,\n    84→      \"application_received\",\n    85→      `${applicantName} applied to join your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    86→    );\n    87→  }\n    88→}\n    89→\n    90→export function notifyApplicationDecision(\n    91→  userId: number,\n    92→  listingId: number,\n    93→  decision: \"approved\" | \"rejected\"\n    94→) {\n    95→  const listing = db\n    96→    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    97→    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string } | undefined;\n    98→\n    99→  if (listing) {\n   100→    const type = decision === \"approved\" ? \"application_approved\" : \"application_rejected\";\n   101→    const message =\n   102→      decision === \"approved\"\n   103→        ? `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was approved!`\n   104→        : `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was not accepted.`;\n   105→    createNotification(userId, listingId, type, message);\n   106→  }\n   107→}\n   108→\n   109→export function notifyGroupFull(listingId: number) {\n   110→  const listing = db\n   111→    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   112→    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string } | undefined;\n   113→  const members = db\n   114→    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   115→    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n   116→\n   117→  if (listing) {\n   118→    const botConfigured = !!process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;\n   119→    const message = botConfigured\n   120→      ? `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The Telegram group will be set up automatically.`\n   121→      : `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The organizer will share a Telegram link soon.`;\n   122→\n   123→    for (const member of members) {\n   124→      createNotification(\n   125→        member.user_id,\n   126→        listingId,\n   127→        \"group_full\",\n   128→        message\n   129→      );\n   130→    }\n   131→  }\n   132→}\n   133→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01C53y9Uu5Cyt8xgnYwq99Ev","session_id":"282376ca-7a0b-4c20-878b-0086a85b681b","uuid":"e358f091-fdcc-4e35-9161-d73d36eac6d9","timestamp":"2026-03-20T11:02:14.969Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01B4sLoeGbm8Q3vad814h8C6","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→interface ReadingRow {\n     6→  id: number;\n     7→  book_title: string;\n     8→  book_author: string;\n     9→  book_cover_url: string;\n    10→  reading_pace: string;\n    11→  start_date: string;\n    12→  meeting_format: string;\n    13→  is_full: number;\n    14→  telegram_link: string;\n    15→  member_count: number;\n    16→  max_group_size: number;\n    17→  author_name: string;\n    18→  joined_at: string;\n    19→}\n    20→\n    21→export async function GET() {\n    22→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    23→  if (!session) {\n    24→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    25→  }\n    26→\n    27→  // Get all listings the user is a member of (including ones they authored)\n    28→  const readings = db.prepare(`\n    29→    SELECT\n    30→      l.id,\n    31→      l.book_title,\n    32→      l.book_author,\n    33→      l.book_cover_url,\n    34→      l.reading_pace,\n    35→      l.start_date,\n    36→      l.meeting_format,\n    37→      l.is_full,\n    38→      l.telegram_link,\n    39→      l.max_group_size,\n    40→      u.display_name as author_name,\n    41→      lm.joined_at,\n    42→      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = l.id) as member_count\n    43→    FROM listing_members lm\n    44→    JOIN listings l ON l.id = lm.listing_id\n    45→    JOIN users u ON u.id = l.author_id\n    46→    WHERE lm.user_id = ?\n    47→    ORDER BY lm.joined_at DESC\n    48→  `).all(session.userId) as ReadingRow[];\n    49→\n    50→  const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n    51→\n    52→  // Currently reading: groups that are full (active) or start_date <= today\n    53→  const currentlyReading = readings.filter(\n    54→    (r) => r.is_full === 1 || r.start_date <= today\n    55→  );\n    56→\n    57→  // Reading history: completed groups (full + start_date in the past)\n    58→  // Upcoming: not yet started and not full\n    59→  const history = readings.filter(\n    60→    (r) => r.is_full === 1 && r.start_date < today\n    61→  );\n    62→\n    63→  const upcoming = readings.filter(\n    64→    (r) => r.is_full === 0 && r.start_date > today\n    65→  );\n    66→\n    67→  const active = readings.filter(\n    68→    (r) => !history.includes(r) && !upcoming.includes(r) && (r.is_full === 1 || r.start_date <= today)\n    69→  );\n    70→\n    71→  return NextResponse.json({\n    72→    active,\n    73→    history,\n    74→    upcoming,\n    75→    total: readings.length,\n    76→  });\n    77→}\n    78→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01C53y9Uu5Cyt8xgnYwq99Ev","session_id":"282376ca-7a0b-4c20-878b-0086a85b681b","uuid":"9cd60141-52fd-4e5e-a4d2-c4623740a100","timestamp":"2026-03-20T11:02:16.193Z"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01Ur9vs82zjkPfNoLHvryEdX","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_015nJu8o6R9Z6T6M3bVWnbt6","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/reputation/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4432,"cache_read_input_tokens":26582,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4432,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":25,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01C53y9Uu5Cyt8xgnYwq99Ev","session_id":"282376ca-7a0b-4c20-878b-0086a85b681b","uuid":"6e436064-72ec-4f75-9d90-10b8e4d13ea6"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_015nJu8o6R9Z6T6M3bVWnbt6","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→/**\n     6→ * GET /api/profile/reputation?userId=X\n     7→ *\n     8→ * Returns a user's reputation score and rating breakdown.\n     9→ * If no userId is provided, returns the current user's reputation.\n    10→ */\n    11→export async function GET(req: NextRequest) {\n    12→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    13→  if (!session) {\n    14→    return NextResponse.json(\n    15→      { error: \"Authentication required\" },\n    16→      { status: 401 }\n    17→    );\n    18→  }\n    19→\n    20→  const userId = req.nextUrl.searchParams.get(\"userId\") || session.userId;\n    21→\n    22→  // Average score\n    23→  const stats = db\n    24→    .prepare(\n    25→      `SELECT\n    26→        COUNT(*) as total_ratings,\n    27→        ROUND(AVG(score), 1) as average_score,\n    28→        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as five_star,\n    29→        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 4 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as four_star,\n    30→        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 3 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as three_star,\n    31→        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as two_star,\n    32→        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as one_star\n    33→       FROM ratings\n    34→       WHERE rated_user_id = ?`\n    35→    )\n    36→    .get(userId) as {\n    37→      total_ratings: number;\n    38→      average_score: number | null;\n    39→      five_star: number;\n    40→      four_star: number;\n    41→      three_star: number;\n    42→      two_star: number;\n    43→      one_star: number;\n    44→    };\n    45→\n    46→  // Number of completed groups\n    47→  const completedGroups = db\n    48→    .prepare(\n    49→      `SELECT COUNT(*) as count\n    50→       FROM listing_members lm\n    51→       JOIN listings l ON lm.listing_id = l.id\n    52→       WHERE lm.user_id = ? AND l.is_full = 1`\n    53→    )\n    54→    .get(userId) as { count: number };\n    55→\n    56→  // Number of groups where this user has given ratings\n    57→  const groupsRated = db\n    58→    .prepare(\n    59→      `SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT listing_id) as count\n    60→       FROM ratings\n    61→       WHERE rater_id = ?`\n    62→    )\n    63→    .get(userId) as { count: number };\n    64→\n    65→  // Recent ratings received (with anonymized feedback)\n    66→  const recentRatings = db\n    67→    .prepare(\n    68→      `SELECT r.score, r.comment, r.created_at, l.book_title\n    69→       FROM ratings r\n    70→       JOIN listings l ON r.listing_id = l.id\n    71→       WHERE r.rated_user_id = ?\n    72→       ORDER BY r.created_at DESC\n    73→       LIMIT 10`\n    74→    )\n    75→    .all(userId) as {\n    76→      score: number;\n    77→      comment: string;\n    78→      created_at: string;\n    79→      book_title: string;\n    80→    }[];\n    81→\n    82→  return NextResponse.json({\n    83→    reputation: {\n    84→      averageScore: stats.average_score || 0,\n    85→      totalRatings: stats.total_ratings,\n    86→      completedGroups: completedGroups.count,\n    87→      groupsRated: groupsRated.count,\n    88→      breakdown: {\n    89→        5: stats.five_star || 0,\n    90→        4: stats.four_star || 0,\n    91→        3: stats.three_star || 0,\n    92→        2: stats.two_star || 0,\n    93→        1: stats.one_star || 0,\n    94→      },\n    95→      recentRatings,\n    96→    },\n    97→  });\n    98→}\n    99→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01C53y9Uu5Cyt8xgnYwq99Ev","session_id":"282376ca-7a0b-4c20-878b-0086a85b681b","uuid":"52e229c6-3941-4679-8198-09acdd88780c","timestamp":"2026-03-20T11:02:19.140Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01PzPT7qNYQZWGmSaXHnpNpx","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import { isBotConfigured, setWebhook, getBotInfo } from \"@/lib/telegram\";\n     3→\n     4→/**\n     5→ * POST /api/telegram/setup\n     6→ *\n     7→ * Sets up the Telegram webhook. Should be called once after deployment.\n     8→ * Requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL env vars.\n     9→ *\n    10→ * Protected by a setup secret to prevent unauthorized access.\n    11→ */\n    12→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n    13→  const setupSecret = process.env.TELEGRAM_SETUP_SECRET;\n    14→  if (!setupSecret) {\n    15→    return NextResponse.json(\n    16→      { error: \"TELEGRAM_SETUP_SECRET not configured\" },\n    17→      { status: 500 }\n    18→    );\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  const body = await req.json();\n    22→  if (body.secret !== setupSecret) {\n    23→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unauthorized\" }, { status: 403 });\n    24→  }\n    25→\n    26→  if (!isBotConfigured()) {\n    27→    return NextResponse.json(\n    28→      { error: \"TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN not configured\" },\n    29→      { status: 500 }\n    30→    );\n    31→  }\n    32→\n    33→  const webhookUrl = process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL;\n    34→  if (!webhookUrl) {\n    35→    return NextResponse.json(\n    36→      { error: \"TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL not configured\" },\n    37→      { status: 500 }\n    38→    );\n    39→  }\n    40→\n    41→  const bot = await getBotInfo();\n    42→  if (!bot) {\n    43→    return NextResponse.json(\n    44→      { error: \"Failed to connect to Telegram API. Check your bot token.\" },\n    45→      { status: 500 }\n    46→    );\n    47→  }\n    48→\n    49→  const success = await setWebhook(webhookUrl);\n    50→  if (!success) {\n    51→    return NextResponse.json(\n    52→      { error: \"Failed to set webhook\" },\n    53→      { status: 500 }\n    54→    );\n    55→  }\n    56→\n    57→  return NextResponse.json({\n    58→    ok: true,\n    59→    bot: { username: bot.username, id: bot.id },\n    60→    webhookUrl,\n    61→  });\n    62→}\n    63→\n    64→/**\n    65→ * GET /api/telegram/setup\n    66→ *\n    67→ * Returns the current Telegram bot configuration status.\n    68→ */\n    69→export async function GET() {\n    70→  return NextResponse.json({\n    71→    botConfigured: isBotConfigured(),\n    72→    webhookUrlConfigured: !!process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL,\n    73→  });\n    74→}\n    75→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01C53y9Uu5Cyt8xgnYwq99Ev","session_id":"282376ca-7a0b-4c20-878b-0086a85b681b","uuid":"070b495f-2d50-4629-90a3-3bb4ee976cc0","timestamp":"2026-03-20T11:02:19.575Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_018rJqGcyrHF9CvraBfSB1D2","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→export async function POST(\n     6→  req: NextRequest,\n     7→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     8→) {\n     9→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    10→  if (!session) {\n    11→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    12→  }\n    13→\n    14→  const { id } = await params;\n    15→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    16→\n    17→  const listing = db\n    18→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    19→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    20→\n    21→  if (!listing) {\n    22→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    23→  }\n    24→\n    25→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json(\n    27→      { error: \"Only the listing author can set the Telegram link\" },\n    28→      { status: 403 }\n    29→    );\n    30→  }\n    31→\n    32→  const { telegramLink } = await req.json();\n    33→\n    34→  if (!telegramLink || !telegramLink.startsWith(\"https://t.me/\")) {\n    35→    return NextResponse.json(\n    36→      { error: \"Please provide a valid Telegram invite link (https://t.me/...)\" },\n    37→      { status: 400 }\n    38→    );\n    39→  }\n    40→\n    41→  db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET telegram_link = ? WHERE id = ?\").run(\n    42→    telegramLink.trim(),\n    43→    listingId\n    44→  );\n    45→\n    46→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n    47→}\n    48→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01C53y9Uu5Cyt8xgnYwq99Ev","session_id":"282376ca-7a0b-4c20-878b-0086a85b681b","uuid":"8bdfe10e-c033-460a-81a8-e213c018f49d","timestamp":"2026-03-20T11:02:20.118Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01MhL9nPpgmVHUdUmTZwV2it","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import nodemailer from \"nodemailer\";\n     2→\n     3→const isConfigured =\n     4→  !!process.env.SMTP_HOST &&\n     5→  !!process.env.SMTP_PORT &&\n     6→  !!process.env.SMTP_FROM;\n     7→\n     8→let transporter: nodemailer.Transporter | null = null;\n     9→\n    10→function getTransporter(): nodemailer.Transporter | null {\n    11→  if (!isConfigured) return null;\n    12→  if (!transporter) {\n    13→    transporter = nodemailer.createTransport({\n    14→      host: process.env.SMTP_HOST,\n    15→      port: parseInt(process.env.SMTP_PORT || \"587\", 10),\n    16→      secure: process.env.SMTP_SECURE === \"true\",\n    17→      ...(process.env.SMTP_USER && process.env.SMTP_PASS\n    18→        ? {\n    19→            auth: {\n    20→              user: process.env.SMTP_USER,\n    21→              pass: process.env.SMTP_PASS,\n    22→            },\n    23→          }\n    24→        : {}),\n    25→    });\n    26→  }\n    27→  return transporter;\n    28→}\n    29→\n    30→export async function sendEmail(\n    31→  to: string,\n    32→  subject: string,\n    33→  text: string,\n    34→  html?: string\n    35→): Promise<boolean> {\n    36→  const t = getTransporter();\n    37→  if (!t) return false;\n    38→\n    39→  try {\n    40→    await t.sendMail({\n    41→      from: process.env.SMTP_FROM,\n    42→      to,\n    43→      subject,\n    44→      text,\n    45→      html: html || text,\n    46→    });\n    47→    return true;\n    48→  } catch (err) {\n    49→    console.error(\"Failed to send email:\", err);\n    50→    return false;\n    51→  }\n    52→}\n    53→\n    54→export function isEmailConfigured(): boolean {\n    55→  return isConfigured;\n    56→}\n    57→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01C53y9Uu5Cyt8xgnYwq99Ev","session_id":"282376ca-7a0b-4c20-878b-0086a85b681b","uuid":"fc4b4774-11e0-4ca2-bf74-0f2496551b24","timestamp":"2026-03-20T11:02:22.658Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Y44hpXfZ3PbKh9xDT9cbET","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { isBotConfigured, generateGroupDeepLink } from \"@/lib/telegram\";\n     5→\n     6→/**\n     7→ * GET /api/listings/[id]/auto-telegram\n     8→ *\n     9→ * Returns the Telegram deep link for one-click group creation.\n    10→ * Only available to the listing author when the group is full\n    11→ * and no Telegram link has been set yet.\n    12→ */\n    13→export async function GET(\n    14→  _req: NextRequest,\n    15→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n    16→) {\n    17→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    18→  if (!session) {\n    19→    return NextResponse.json(\n    20→      { error: \"Authentication required\" },\n    21→      { status: 401 }\n    22→    );\n    23→  }\n    24→\n    25→  if (!isBotConfigured()) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json(\n    27→      { error: \"Telegram bot not configured\", botConfigured: false },\n    28→      { status: 404 }\n    29→    );\n    30→  }\n    31→\n    32→  const { id } = await params;\n    33→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    34→\n    35→  const listing = db\n    36→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    37→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    38→\n    39→  if (!listing) {\n    40→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    41→  }\n    42→\n    43→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    44→    return NextResponse.json(\n    45→      { error: \"Only the organizer can create the Telegram group\" },\n    46→      { status: 403 }\n    47→    );\n    48→  }\n    49→\n    50→  if (!listing.is_full) {\n    51→    return NextResponse.json(\n    52→      { error: \"Group must be full before creating a Telegram group\" },\n    53→      { status: 400 }\n    54→    );\n    55→  }\n    56→\n    57→  if (listing.telegram_link) {\n    58→    return NextResponse.json(\n    59→      { error: \"Telegram link already set\", telegramLink: listing.telegram_link },\n    60→      { status: 400 }\n    61→    );\n    62→  }\n    63→\n    64→  const deepLink = await generateGroupDeepLink(listingId);\n    65→  if (!deepLink) {\n    66→    return NextResponse.json(\n    67→      { error: \"Failed to generate deep link. Check bot configuration.\" },\n    68→      { status: 500 }\n    69→    );\n    70→  }\n    71→\n    72→  return NextResponse.json({\n    73→    deepLink,\n    74→    instructions:\n    75→      \"Click the link to open Telegram and create a group. The bot will automatically generate an invite link for your reading group.\",\n    76→  });\n    77→}\n    78→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01C53y9Uu5Cyt8xgnYwq99Ev","session_id":"282376ca-7a0b-4c20-878b-0086a85b681b","uuid":"4b53762c-4f02-454a-bcc3-fe66dd82fe22","timestamp":"2026-03-20T11:02:23.119Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01P4cY6zyQqGM2UHHDuaT4b3","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import { isBotConfigured, getBotInfo } from \"@/lib/discord\";\n     3→\n     4→/**\n     5→ * Discord bot setup endpoint.\n     6→ * Returns bot info and invite URL for verification.\n     7→ *\n     8→ * Protected by DISCORD_SETUP_SECRET env var.\n     9→ */\n    10→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n    11→  const { secret } = await req.json();\n    12→  const setupSecret = process.env.DISCORD_SETUP_SECRET || \"\";\n    13→\n    14→  if (!setupSecret || secret !== setupSecret) {\n    15→    return NextResponse.json(\n    16→      { error: \"Invalid setup secret\" },\n    17→      { status: 403 }\n    18→    );\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  if (!isBotConfigured()) {\n    22→    return NextResponse.json(\n    23→      {\n    24→        error:\n    25→          \"Discord bot is not configured. Set DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN and DISCORD_CLIENT_ID environment variables.\",\n    26→      },\n    27→      { status: 503 }\n    28→    );\n    29→  }\n    30→\n    31→  const botInfo = await getBotInfo();\n    32→  if (!botInfo) {\n    33→    return NextResponse.json(\n    34→      { error: \"Failed to connect to Discord API. Check your bot token.\" },\n    35→      { status: 500 }\n    36→    );\n    37→  }\n    38→\n    39→  return NextResponse.json({\n    40→    ok: true,\n    41→    bot: {\n    42→      id: botInfo.id,\n    43→      username: botInfo.username,\n    44→    },\n    45→    message: `Discord bot \"${botInfo.username}\" is configured and ready.`,\n    46→  });\n    47→}\n    48→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01C53y9Uu5Cyt8xgnYwq99Ev","session_id":"282376ca-7a0b-4c20-878b-0086a85b681b","uuid":"6a123831-92a7-4e32-8384-d72767193779","timestamp":"2026-03-20T11:02:23.494Z"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_0156MwuLdD2g1NSAMDb4ZKrh","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01ScsHwwBXv32Bm6Y9VcPEYL","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/genres/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2252,"cache_read_input_tokens":34003,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":2252,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":25,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01C53y9Uu5Cyt8xgnYwq99Ev","session_id":"282376ca-7a0b-4c20-878b-0086a85b681b","uuid":"faa2706e-a056-49ff-89af-052b7a5ed31c"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01ScsHwwBXv32Bm6Y9VcPEYL","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→const AVAILABLE_GENRES = [\n     6→  \"Literary Fiction\",\n     7→  \"Science Fiction\",\n     8→  \"Fantasy\",\n     9→  \"Mystery\",\n    10→  \"Thriller\",\n    11→  \"Romance\",\n    12→  \"Historical Fiction\",\n    13→  \"Horror\",\n    14→  \"Dystopian\",\n    15→  \"Adventure\",\n    16→  \"Contemporary Fiction\",\n    17→  \"Magical Realism\",\n    18→  \"Classics\",\n    19→  \"Short Stories\",\n    20→  \"Young Adult\",\n    21→  \"Graphic Novels\",\n    22→];\n    23→\n    24→interface GenreRow {\n    25→  genre: string;\n    26→}\n    27→\n    28→export async function GET() {\n    29→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    30→  if (!session) {\n    31→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    32→  }\n    33→\n    34→  const userGenres = db.prepare(\n    35→    \"SELECT genre FROM user_genres WHERE user_id = ? ORDER BY genre\"\n    36→  ).all(session.userId) as GenreRow[];\n    37→\n    38→  return NextResponse.json({\n    39→    genres: userGenres.map((g) => g.genre),\n    40→    available: AVAILABLE_GENRES,\n    41→  });\n    42→}\n    43→\n    44→export async function PUT(req: NextRequest) {\n    45→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    46→  if (!session) {\n    47→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    48→  }\n    49→\n    50→  const { genres } = await req.json();\n    51→\n    52→  if (!Array.isArray(genres)) {\n    53→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"genres must be an array\" }, { status: 400 });\n    54→  }\n    55→\n    56→  // Validate all genres\n    57→  const validGenres = genres.filter((g: string) => AVAILABLE_GENRES.includes(g));\n    58→\n    59→  // Replace all genres in a transaction\n    60→  const updateGenres = db.transaction(() => {\n    61→    db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM user_genres WHERE user_id = ?\").run(session.userId);\n    62→    const insert = db.prepare(\"INSERT INTO user_genres (user_id, genre) VALUES (?, ?)\");\n    63→    for (const genre of validGenres) {\n    64→      insert.run(session.userId, genre);\n    65→    }\n    66→  });\n    67→\n    68→  updateGenres();\n    69→\n    70→  return NextResponse.json({ genres: validGenres });\n    71→}\n    72→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01C53y9Uu5Cyt8xgnYwq99Ev","session_id":"282376ca-7a0b-4c20-878b-0086a85b681b","uuid":"10a80593-ac18-4520-b9ad-0fdeeae300c4","timestamp":"2026-03-20T11:02:26.358Z"}

  2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { getSession } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { isBotConfigured, generateBotInviteUrl } from \"@/lib/discord\";\n     5→\n     6→export async const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    27→\n    28→  const listing = db\n    29→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    30→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    31→\n    32→  if (!listing) {\n    33→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    34→  }\n    35→\n    36→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    37→    return NextResponse.json(\n    38→      { error: \"Only the listing author can set up the Discord group\" },\n    39→      { status: 403 }\n    40→    );\n    41→  }\n    42→\n    43→  if (!listing.is_full) {\n    44→    return NextResponse.json(\n    45→      { error: \"The group is not full yet\" },\n    46→      { status: 400 }\n    47→    );\n    48→  }\n    49→\n    50→  if (listing.discord_link) {\n    51→    return NextResponse.json(\n    52→      { error: \"A Discord link has already been set\" },\n    53→      { status: 400 }\n    54→    );\n    55→  }\n    56→\n    57→  const inviteUrl = generateBotInviteUrl(listingId);\n    58→\n    59→  return NextResponse.json({\n    60→    inviteUrl,\n    61→    instructions:\n    62→      \"Click the link to add the Bookmate bot to your Discord server. \" +\n    63→      \"The bot will create a reading group channel and generate an invite link for your members.\",\n    64→  });\n    65→}\n    66→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01C53y9Uu5Cyt8xgnYwq99Ev","session_id":"282376ca-7a0b-4c20-878b-0086a85b681b","uuid":"da2fbd80-11b9-45af-ac37-688086c5051a","timestamp":"2026-03-20T11:02:26.822Z"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"task_notification","task_id":"a6c6355156d4cb6f6","tool_use_id":"toolu_01C53y9Uu5Cyt8xgnYwq99Ev","status":"completed","output_file":"","summary":"Review codebase for issues","usage":{"total_tokens":38583,"tool_uses":27,"duration_ms":84950},"uuid":"eafbfa0b-3083-462c-8d3b-8d4f4256e11c","session_id":"282376ca-7a0b-4c20-878b-0086a85b681b"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01C53y9Uu5Cyt8xgnYwq99Ev","type":"tool_result","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Now I have a thorough picture of the entire codebase. Let me compile the findings.\n\n---\n\n## Code Review: Bookmate Next.js Application\n\nReviewing all API routes in `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/`, auth/session logic in `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/session.ts`, and database layer in `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/db.ts`.\n\n---\n\n## Critical Issues\n\n### 1. Hardcoded Fallback Session Secret in Production\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/session.ts`, line 12\n\n**Confidence: 95**\n\nThe `SESSION_SECRET` falls back to a well-known, publicly visible string if the environment variable is not set:\n\n```ts\npassword:\n  process.env.SESSION_SECRET ||\n  \"bookmate-development-secret-key-change-in-production-32chars!\",\n```\n\niron-session uses this password to encrypt and sign cookies. If `SESSION_SECRET` is not set in a production deployment, any attacker who has read this source code (e.g., from a public repo) can forge arbitrary session cookies, giving them full unauthenticated access as any `userId`. The app should throw a hard startup error instead of silently using a known fallback.\n\n**Fix:** Replace the fallback with a startup-time assertion:\n\n```ts\nconst secret = process.env.SESSION_SECRET;\nif (!secret) {\n  throw new Error(\"SESSION_SECRET environment variable is required\");\n}\nconst sessionOptions: SessionOptions = {\n  password: secret,\n  ...\n};\n```\n\n---\n\n### 2. IDOR: Any Authenticated User Can View Any User's Reputation and Private Ratings\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/reputation/route.ts`, lines 20–36\n\n**Confidence: 88**\n\nThe `userId` query parameter is taken directly from the request without sanitising the type, and the route passes it straight into the SQL query:\n\n```ts\nconst userId = req.nextUrl.searchParams.get(\"userId\") || session.userId;\n// ...\n.get(userId)   // userId is a string here, not a number\n```\n\nThere are two compounding problems:\n\n- **Type mismatch / potential injection surface:** `searchParams.get()` returns a `string`. This is passed directly to `better-sqlite3`'s `.get()` as the binding for `WHERE rated_user_id = ?`. While better-sqlite3's parameterised queries prevent classic SQL injection, the integer comparison in SQLite coerces the string, so it will accidentally work — but this is relying on implicit type coercion, and the intent is clearly to use an integer.\n\n- **No authorisation check:** The route is only auth-gated (any logged-in user can call it), but `recentRatings` (lines 68–80) exposes every `comment` left on the target user. Rating comments are entered by peers and could be personal or sensitive. There is no check that the requesting user has a legitimate relationship (e.g., shared group membership) with the `userId` being queried.\n\n**Fix:** Parse and validate `userId` as an integer and, if querying another user, restrict `recentRatings` to anonymous summaries or require group membership.\n\n---\n\n### 3. Race Condition: Join Endpoint Allows Exceeding `max_group_size`\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`, lines 48–72\n\n**Confidence: 90**\n\nThe \"check current count then insert\" logic is not wrapped in a transaction or protected by a database-level lock:\n\n```ts\n// Count current members\nconst { count } = db.prepare(...).get(listingId) as { count: number };\n\nif (count >= listing.max_group_size) { ... }\n\n// Join  <-- another request can have passed the count check simultaneously\ndb.prepare(\"INSERT INTO listing_members ...\").run(listingId, session.userId);\n```\n\nTwo concurrent requests can both read `count = max_group_size - 1`, both pass the guard, and both insert — resulting in a group with `max_group_size + 1` members. The `applications` route correctly uses `db.transaction()` for the equivalent approval path (line 58 of `applications/route.ts`), but the direct-join path does not.\n\n**Fix:** Wrap the count check and INSERT in a `db.transaction()`, identical to the pattern already used in the applications route.\n\n---\n\n### 4. Unauthenticated Discord Webhook Accepts Arbitrary Listing Links\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/discord/webhook/route.ts`, lines 21–44\n\n**Confidence: 85**\n\nThe Discord webhook endpoint has no authentication or secret verification:\n\n```ts\nexport async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n  try {\n    const body = await req.json();\n    const { type } = body;\n    if (type === \"link\") {\n      return handleLink(body);  // no auth check before this\n    }\n```\n\nThe `handleLink` function (line 46) accepts a `listingId` from the request body and calls `createTextChannel`, `createChannelInvite`, and `sendChannelMessage` on the Discord API — operations that consume bot quota and can create channels in any server the bot has been added to. Any external party who discovers this endpoint URL can trigger unlimited Discord API calls and create unwanted channels.\n\nCompare this to the Telegram webhook route, which conditionally checks `X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token` (lines 26–30 of `telegram/webhook/route.ts`). The Discord webhook has no equivalent guard at all.\n\n**Fix:** Add a `DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET` environment variable check at the top of the POST handler, similar to the Telegram webhook implementation. For Discord Interaction endpoints specifically, the correct approach is verifying the `Ed25519` signature using the `DISCORD_PUBLIC_KEY`.\n\n---\n\n## Important Issues\n\n### 5. No Input Length Limits on Free-Text Fields\n\n**Files:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/auth/register/route.ts` (lines 8–15), `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/create/route.ts` (lines 13–26), `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/route.ts` (lines 11–20)\n\n**Confidence: 82**\n\nFree-text fields like `displayName`, `bio`, `bookTitle`, `bookAuthor`, `readingPace`, and `comment` (in ratings) are accepted and stored without any maximum length validation. SQLite has no column-level `VARCHAR(n)` enforcement — it stores whatever is inserted. A malicious or erroneous client can submit megabytes of text per field, bloating the database and potentially causing downstream rendering issues.\n\nThe `password` field checks a minimum length of 8 but has no maximum, which creates a denial-of-service vector: bcrypt's cost scales with input length, and extremely long passwords will block the Node.js event loop for multiple seconds per request (bcrypt with a 1 MB password is measurably slow even at cost 12).\n\n**Fix:** Add explicit `maxLength` guards before processing. For passwords specifically, reject inputs longer than 72 characters (bcrypt's effective limit anyway). For display names and bio, enforce reasonable limits (e.g., 100 and 500 characters).\n\n---\n\n### 6. `/link` Telegram Command Links Groups Without Author Verification\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts`, lines 173–208\n\n**Confidence: 83**\n\nThe `/link` command handler finds unlinked full listings and, if there is only one, auto-links the Telegram chat to it without verifying that the person issuing the command in the Telegram group is actually the Bookmate author of that listing:\n\n```ts\nif (unlinkedListings.length === 1) {\n  // Auto-link the only available listing\n  await linkTelegramToListing(unlinkedListings[0].id, message.chat.id);\n  return;\n}\n```\n\nThis means anyone who adds the bot to any Telegram group and types `/link` can claim the next available completed listing, overwriting its `telegram_link` and sending invite notifications to all members of that group. An attacker controlling a Telegram group can hijack recently completed reading groups.\n\n**Fix:** Require the Bookmate user account to be linked to a Telegram user ID before allowing `/link` to work, or at minimum require the command sender's Telegram ID to match the `from.id` stored during the auto-telegram deep-link flow."},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: a6c6355156d4cb6f6 (use SendMessage with to: 'a6c6355156d4cb6f6' to continue this agent)\n<usage>total_tokens: 40028\ntool_uses: 27\nduration_ms: 84952</usage>"}]}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"282376ca-7a0b-4c20-878b-0086a85b681b","uuid":"cfb61a4d-4557-41cb-ac9e-01a71105a37d","timestamp":"2026-03-20T11:03:10.597Z","tool_use_result":{"status":"completed","prompt":"Review the Bookmate Next.js application in /root/Projects/bookmate/app for bugs, security issues, and code quality problems. Focus on:\n1. API routes in app/api/ - especially auth, input validation, SQL injection risks\n2. Session/auth security in lib/session.ts\n3. Database schema and queries in lib/db.ts\n4. Any missing error handling or edge cases\n\nThis is a Next.js 15 app with SQLite (better-sqlite3), iron-session auth, and Tailwind CSS. Only report HIGH confidence issues that truly matter.","agentId":"a6c6355156d4cb6f6","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Now I have a thorough picture of the entire codebase. Let me compile the findings.\n\n---\n\n## Code Review: Bookmate Next.js Application\n\nReviewing all API routes in `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/`, auth/session logic in `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/session.ts`, and database layer in `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/db.ts`.\n\n---\n\n## Critical Issues\n\n### 1. Hardcoded Fallback Session Secret in Production\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/session.ts`, line 12\n\n**Confidence: 95**\n\nThe `SESSION_SECRET` falls back to a well-known, publicly visible string if the environment variable is not set:\n\n```ts\npassword:\n  process.env.SESSION_SECRET ||\n  \"bookmate-development-secret-key-change-in-production-32chars!\",\n```\n\niron-session uses this password to encrypt and sign cookies. If `SESSION_SECRET` is not set in a production deployment, any attacker who has read this source code (e.g., from a public repo) can forge arbitrary session cookies, giving them full unauthenticated access as any `userId`. The app should throw a hard startup error instead of silently using a known fallback.\n\n**Fix:** Replace the fallback with a startup-time assertion:\n\n```ts\nconst secret = process.env.SESSION_SECRET;\nif (!secret) {\n  throw new Error(\"SESSION_SECRET environment variable is required\");\n}\nconst sessionOptions: SessionOptions = {\n  password: secret,\n  ...\n};\n```\n\n---\n\n### 2. IDOR: Any Authenticated User Can View Any User's Reputation and Private Ratings\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/reputation/route.ts`, lines 20–36\n\n**Confidence: 88**\n\nThe `userId` query parameter is taken directly from the request without sanitising the type, and the route passes it straight into the SQL query:\n\n```ts\nconst userId = req.nextUrl.searchParams.get(\"userId\") || session.userId;\n// ...\n.get(userId)   // userId is a string here, not a number\n```\n\nThere are two compounding problems:\n\n- **Type mismatch / potential injection surface:** `searchParams.get()` returns a `string`. This is passed directly to `better-sqlite3`'s `.get()` as the binding for `WHERE rated_user_id = ?`. While better-sqlite3's parameterised queries prevent classic SQL injection, the integer comparison in SQLite coerces the string, so it will accidentally work — but this is relying on implicit type coercion, and the intent is clearly to use an integer.\n\n- **No authorisation check:** The route is only auth-gated (any logged-in user can call it), but `recentRatings` (lines 68–80) exposes every `comment` left on the target user. Rating comments are entered by peers and could be personal or sensitive. There is no check that the requesting user has a legitimate relationship (e.g., shared group membership) with the `userId` being queried.\n\n**Fix:** Parse and validate `userId` as an integer and, if querying another user, restrict `recentRatings` to anonymous summaries or require group membership.\n\n---\n\n### 3. Race Condition: Join Endpoint Allows Exceeding `max_group_size`\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`, lines 48–72\n\n**Confidence: 90**\n\nThe \"check current count then insert\" logic is not wrapped in a transaction or protected by a database-level lock:\n\n```ts\n// Count current members\nconst { count } = db.prepare(...).get(listingId) as { count: number };\n\nif (count >= listing.max_group_size) { ... }\n\n// Join  <-- another request can have passed the count check simultaneously\ndb.prepare(\"INSERT INTO listing_members ...\").run(listingId, session.userId);\n```\n\nTwo concurrent requests can both read `count = max_group_size - 1`, both pass the guard, and both insert — resulting in a group with `max_group_size + 1` members. The `applications` route correctly uses `db.transaction()` for the equivalent approval path (line 58 of `applications/route.ts`), but the direct-join path does not.\n\n**Fix:** Wrap the count check and INSERT in a `db.transaction()`, identical to the pattern already used in the applications route.\n\n---\n\n### 4. Unauthenticated Discord Webhook Accepts Arbitrary Listing Links\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/discord/webhook/route.ts`, lines 21–44\n\n**Confidence: 85**\n\nThe Discord webhook endpoint has no authentication or secret verification:\n\n```ts\nexport async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n  try {\n    const body = await req.json();\n    const { type } = body;\n    if (type === \"link\") {\n      return handleLink(body);  // no auth check before this\n    }\n```\n\nThe `handleLink` function (line 46) accepts a `listingId` from the request body and calls `createTextChannel`, `createChannelInvite`, and `sendChannelMessage` on the Discord API — operations that consume bot quota and can create channels in any server the bot has been added to. Any external party who discovers this endpoint URL can trigger unlimited Discord API calls and create unwanted channels.\n\nCompare this to the Telegram webhook route, which conditionally checks `X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token` (lines 26–30 of `telegram/webhook/route.ts`). The Discord webhook has no equivalent guard at all.\n\n**Fix:** Add a `DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET` environment variable check at the top of the POST handler, similar to the Telegram webhook implementation. For Discord Interaction endpoints specifically, the correct approach is verifying the `Ed25519` signature using the `DISCORD_PUBLIC_KEY`.\n\n---\n\n## Important Issues\n\n### 5. No Input Length Limits on Free-Text Fields\n\n**Files:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/auth/register/route.ts` (lines 8–15), `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/create/route.ts` (lines 13–26), `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/route.ts` (lines 11–20)\n\n**Confidence: 82**\n\nFree-text fields like `displayName`, `bio`, `bookTitle`, `bookAuthor`, `readingPace`, and `comment` (in ratings) are accepted and stored without any maximum length validation. SQLite has no column-level `VARCHAR(n)` enforcement — it stores whatever is inserted. A malicious or erroneous client can submit megabytes of text per field, bloating the database and potentially causing downstream rendering issues.\n\nThe `password` field checks a minimum length of 8 but has no maximum, which creates a denial-of-service vector: bcrypt's cost scales with input length, and extremely long passwords will block the Node.js event loop for multiple seconds per request (bcrypt with a 1 MB password is measurably slow even at cost 12).\n\n**Fix:** Add explicit `maxLength` guards before processing. For passwords specifically, reject inputs longer than 72 characters (bcrypt's effective limit anyway). For display names and bio, enforce reasonable limits (e.g., 100 and 500 characters).\n\n---\n\n### 6. `/link` Telegram Command Links Groups Without Author Verification\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts`, lines 173–208\n\n**Confidence: 83**\n\nThe `/link` command handler finds unlinked full listings and, if there is only one, auto-links the Telegram chat to it without verifying that the person issuing the command in the Telegram group is actually the Bookmate author of that listing:\n\n```ts\nif (unlinkedListings.length === 1) {\n  // Auto-link the only available listing\n  await linkTelegramToListing(unlinkedListings[0].id, message.chat.id);\n  return;\n}\n```\n\nThis means anyone who adds the bot to any Telegram group and types `/link` can claim the next available completed listing, overwriting its `telegram_link` and sending invite notifications to all members of that group. An attacker controlling a Telegram group can hijack recently completed reading groups.\n\n**Fix:** Require the Bookmate user account to be linked to a Telegram user ID before allowing `/link` to work, or at minimum require the command sender's Telegram ID to match the `from.id` stored during the auto-telegram deep-link flow."}],"totalDurationMs":84952,"totalTokens":40028,"totalToolUseCount":27,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1712,"cache_read_input_tokens":36255,"output_tokens":2060,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1712},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_016Xg1AidwM6Ekc1x4yfE9nk","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_017KmP2KBazWsxweEutkQWnS","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2) && gh issue create --title \"Security & correctness fixes: session secret, race condition, webhook auth, input validation\" --label \"bug,approved,P0\" --body \"$(cat <<'EOF'\n## Issues Found via Code Review\n\n1. **Hardcoded session secret fallback** — if SESSION_SECRET env var is missing, the app silently uses a publicly known string, allowing session forgery\n2. **Race condition on join endpoint** — concurrent requests can exceed max_group_size (join path doesn't use a transaction, unlike the approval path)\n3. **Unauthenticated Discord webhook** — no secret verification, allowing external parties to create channels\n4. **No input length limits** — passwords can be megabytes (bcrypt DoS), free-text fields unbounded\n5. **IDOR on reputation endpoint** — any user can view any other user's private ratings/comments\n6. **Telegram /link without author verification** — anyone can link a Telegram group to a listing\n\n## Priority\nAll are security or correctness bugs. Fixing #1-4 immediately; #5-6 lower risk.\nEOF\n)\"","description":"Create security bug issue"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2663,"cache_read_input_tokens":23955,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":2663},"output_tokens":10,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"282376ca-7a0b-4c20-878b-0086a85b681b","uuid":"b4a20b92-4f48-4006-8b4b-87d56ae92ad6"}

\"iron-session\";\n     2→import { cookies } from \"next/headers\";\n     3→\n     4→export interface SessionData {\n     5→  userId?: number;\n     6→  email?: string;\n     7→  displayName?: string;\n     8→}\n     9→\n    10→const sessionOptions: SessionOptions = {\n    11→  password:\n    12→    process.env.SESSION_SECRET ||\n    13→    \"bookmate-development-secret-key-change-in-production-32chars!\",\n    14→  cookieName: \"bookmate_session\",\n    15→  cookieOptions: {\n    16→    httpOnly: true,\n    17→    secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\",\n    18→    sameSite: \"lax\",\n    19→    maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 7, // 1 week\n    20→  },\n    21→};\n    22→\n    23→export async function getSession() {\n    24→  const cookieStore = await cookies();\n    25→  return getIronSession<SessionData>(cookieStore, sessionOptions);\n    26→}\n    27→\n    28→export async function requireAuth() {\n    29→  const session = await getSession();\n    30→  if (!session.userId) {\n    31→    return null;\n    32→  }\n    33→  return session;\n    34→}\n    35→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"282376ca-7a0b-4c20-878b-0086a85b681b","uuid":"9af9a0cf-aadb-4ef2-b570-da20a91bdc78","timestamp":"2026-03-20T11:03:35.296Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/session.ts","content":"import { getIronSession, type SessionOptions } from \"iron-session\";\nimport { cookies } from \"next/headers\";\n\nexport interface SessionData {\n  userId?: number;\n  email?: string;\n  displayName?: string;\n}\n\nconst sessionOptions: SessionOptions = {\n  password:\n    process.env.SESSION_SECRET ||\n    \"bookmate-development-secret-key-change-in-production-32chars!\",\n  cookieName: \"bookmate_session\",\n  cookieOptions: {\n    httpOnly: true,\n    secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\",\n    sameSite: \"lax\",\n    maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 7, // 1 week\n  },\n};\n\nexport async function getSession() {\n  const cookieStore = await cookies();\n  return getIronSession<SessionData>(cookieStore, sessionOptions);\n}\n\nexport async function requireAuth() {\n  const session = await getSession();\n  if (!session.userId) {\n    return null;\n  }\n  return session;\n}\n","numLines":35,"startLine":1,"totalLines":35}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01XxLiyzX8ZFJudGjRvt8GcZ","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyListingAuthor, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(\n     7→  _req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→\n    18→  const listing = db\n    19→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    20→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    21→\n    22→  if (!listing) {\n    23→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    24→  }\n    25→\n    26→  if (listing.requires_approval) {\n    27→    return NextResponse.json(\n    28→      { error: \"This group requires approval. Please apply instead.\" },\n    29→      { status: 400 }\n    30→    );\n    31→  }\n    32→\n    33→  if (listing.is_full) {\n    34→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n    35→  }\n    36→\n    37→  // Check if already a member\n    38→  const existing = db\n    39→    .prepare(\n    40→      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    41→    )\n    42→    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    43→\n    44→  if (existing) {\n    45→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are already a member\" }, { status: 400 });\n    46→  }\n    47→\n    48→  // Count current members\n    49→  const { count } = db\n    50→    .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    51→    .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n    52→\n    53→  if (count >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    54→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n    55→  }\n    56→\n    57→  // Join\n    58→  db.prepare(\n    59→    \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    60→  ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n    61→\n    62→  // Notify the author\n    63→  notifyListingAuthor(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n    64→\n    65→  // Check if group is now full\n    66→  const newCount = count + 1;\n    67→  if (newCount >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    68→    db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    69→    notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n    70→  }\n    71→\n    72→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, memberCount: newCount });\n    73→}\n    74→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"282376ca-7a0b-4c20-878b-0086a85b681b","uuid":"fda5a789-7c98-43f2-be44-d54da25a07c6","timestamp":"2026-03-20T11:03:35.736Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\nimport { notifyListingAuthor, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n\nexport async function POST(\n  _req: NextRequest,\n  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n) {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  const { id } = await params;\n  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n\n  if (!listing) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n  }\n\n  if (listing.requires_approval) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"This group requires approval. Please apply instead.\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  if (listing.is_full) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  // Check if already a member\n  const existing = db\n    .prepare(\n      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    )\n    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n\n  if (existing) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are already a member\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  // Count current members\n  const { count } = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n\n  if (count >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  // Join\n  db.prepare(\n    \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n  ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n\n  // Notify the author\n  notifyListingAuthor(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n\n  // Check if group is now full\n  const newCount = count + 1;\n  if (newCount >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n  }\n\n  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, memberCount: newCount });\n}\n","numLines":74,"startLine":1,"totalLines":74}}}
